offer.what: B2B commercial cleaning services on recurring janitorial contracts — offices, medical facilities, commercial kitchens, HOAs, shopping centers, schools, and specialty floor care. Custom proposals after a free site inspection.
offer.coreOutcome: A consistently clean, professional facility every single time — so facilities managers and business owners can stop worrying about their vendor and focus on running their business.
offer.pricePoint: Custom-quoted; no published pricing. Mid-to-high ticket commercial retainer billed on contract terms (daily / weekly / monthly service frequency).
offer.commitment: Recurring janitorial contract; cleaning operations run 24/7 by contract
leadMagnets: Free site inspection and custom cleaning proposal
10% off first service (referenced in directory listings)

audience.icp: Facilities directors, property managers, and business owners operating commercial spaces in South Florida (Boca Raton / Miami area) and select multi-state markets (TX, DC, MD, VA, AR). Typically responsible for vendor selection and day-to-day operations of their facility.
painPoints: Prior cleaning company was unreliable or inconsistent — the 'switching from a bad vendor' narrative
Distrust of cleaning staff entering the facility unsupervised
Disruption to business hours from cleaning schedules
Compliance anxiety for medical/food-service environments (OSHA, CDC)
Specialty floor or kitchen equipment not handled correctly by generalist cleaners

salesCycle.length: Days to weeks — contact form or phone call → site inspection → custom proposal → signed contract
salesCycle.trial: Free site inspection and custom proposal (no paid trial; first engagement is the contract itself)
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Custom written proposal delivered after in-person or virtual site inspection; deal closes when contract is signed
salesCycle.topObjection: Trust and reliability — decision-makers are nervous about letting a new vendor into their facility and worried about repeating a bad experience with a prior cleaning company

ahaMoment: The first morning a new client walks into their facility and finds it immaculate — the visceral before/after that confirms switching vendors was the right call (echoed in multiple testimonials: 'every morning when I walk in my facility looks phenomenal').

retention.recurring: Recurring janitorial contracts are the core revenue model — daily, weekly, or monthly schedules. 98% client retention rate cited on the testimonials page.
retention.expansion: Upsell from base janitorial to specialty floor care (stripping, waxing, carpet extraction), commercial kitchen cleaning, or ad-hoc special services
retention.churnRisk: Quality or consistency slip — a single bad service or missed visit erodes the trust that won the contract; secondary risk is a competitor undercutting on price

lifecycleGaps: No welcome / onboarding email sequence for newly signed contract clients
No lead-magnet nurture for quote inquiries that don't convert immediately
No post-inspection follow-up / proposal conversion sequence
No pre-service reminders or booking confirmation emails
No educational value newsletter for prospects and clients
No renewal, anniversary, or expansion campaign for long-term clients
No win-back / re-engagement campaign for lapsed inquiries or churned clients
toneVoice: Professional, trustworthy, and warm. Confident without being salesy. Copy leans on reliability and tailored service ('every contract is tailored to your specific requirements'). Accessible and responsive — 'we typically reply within 2 hours'. Avoids jargon; speaks plainly to busy operations-minded buyers.
compliance: OSHA and CDC sanitation standards apply for medical facility clients. Standard CAN-SPAM / GDPR email compliance required. No explicit HIPAA language on site but healthcare client segment warrants careful data handling in email copy. No financial or legal sector constraints noted.
